The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIE LEE HUGHES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The settlement agreement contains CCP 664.6 language re: retention by this Probate court; however, no such request was made in the petition. 2) The terms of the settlement agreement include agreement as to statutory and extraordinary fees and costs in the Estate of Willie Hughes, but those amounts have not yet been approved by the court. 3) The petition for final distribution requests extraordinary fees for attorney. Court to determine reasonable of fee request, including to support request for extraordinary fees. 4) The petition for final distribution includes statements from the personal representative that he did not deposit rental income into an estate account until 1 year after his appointment and that he withdrew money for personal expenses totaling $7,873.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA CO.** IN THE MATTER OF THE AMARO LALAND ROMERO MINOR'S SETTLEMENT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds, And Changing Venue And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/28/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the civil court's order approving the minor's compromise is not on file. 2) The initial bond ordered by the civil court is not on file. 3) The petition requests the appointment of Herb Thomas as trustee and periodic payments of his compensation on account, but neither requests were included in the caption or notice of the hearing. See CRC 7.50 & 7.102. 4) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The minor's age, health, and living situation must be described. B) The petitioner has not been appointed as guardian ad litem for the minor in this trust proceeding. Does the petitioner have standing to bring this petition? C) The trust instrument must prohibit modification or revocation without court approval. CRC 7.903(c)(2). Even though the trust instrument states that it is an irrevocable trust, it does not necessarily mean modification or revocation always requires court approval. D) The trust instrument allows any trustee to resign by giving 30-day notice, but the resignation must require court approval. CRC 7.903(c)(7). E) The statute referred to in Article 8-3(f) of the trust instrument regarding a common trust fund is incorrect. F) Why is the petitioner proposing a fiduciary whose residence or usual place of business is not in San Francisco County? G) Does the proposed trustee compensation on account satisfy all the requirements of Probate Code § 2643.1(b)? H) All the factors set forth in Probate Code § 2574(b) must be addressed for the proposed investments in mutual funds and long-term government bonds. I) A copy of a statement or invoice from which the preschool tuition may be verified must be filed. J) Do the minor's parents financially contribute to the preschool tuition? K) What is an estimated total amount of the preschool tuition sought to be paid out of the minor's trust? L) A budget of anticipated expenditures to be paid out of the minor's trust must be provided. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.
